name: Stacey Abrams
subject: The same voter suppression tactics that were used during the Jim Crow era are being used today.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stacey Abrams for Governor
body:
|
---
[](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa9a3/34a064cc/d1dd8ab/70ef4dc/2783328637/VEsH/ "https://staceyabrams.com/")

| | | Friends - Progress is possible, but it is fragile - and across our country, the battles for our most basic civil rights rage on. For example, you may have recently heard about Randolph County, Georgia. Located in the southwest region of the state, Randolph's poverty rate is far north of the national average. The area is mostly rural, predominantly Black, and frequently forgotten. Last week, the Randolph County Board of Elections proposed closing seven of its nine polling locations before November. **In areas with no public transportation, some residents would be forced to walk over three hours to exercise their constitutional right to vote.** This is unacceptable, but we've seen these voter suppression tactics before. We live in a nation that spent centuries denying the right to vote to the poor, to women, and to people of color. Georgia then spent decades creating barriers to prevent these historically-disenfranchised communities from exercising their constitutional right. But a proposal to eliminate nearly every polling location in a Black Belt county belongs in a history textbook, not the current events section. **So, how did we end up here? In short, elections have consequences.** Five years ago, the Supreme Court struck down the beating heart and enforcement arm of the Voting Rights Act of 1965. The court's ruling in Shelby v. Holder allows state and local governments with a history of voting rights violations to pass voting laws at their own discretion. Election boards, like the one in Randolph County, have carte blanche to eliminate polling locations, change election rules, and propose unfair maps - just as they did before 1965. With Shelby, bad actors were given the head of our democracy on a golden platter - the unprecedented ability to silence voters in small towns and rural counties where the fewest number of people are watching. **But here's the thing: WE are watching, and we're fighting back.** Because we know that our ancestors fought too hard and bled too much for us to cede our right to vote just because it is hard. This issue is extremely personal for me. I grew up hearing my parents' stories about how they had to fight for their right to vote in the Jim Crow South. They registered voters in Mississippi at a time when doing so was dangerous, and their bravery influenced my decision to create the New Georgia Project to register every eligible voter in the state. When I founded the New Georgia Project in 2014, there were 800,000 Georgians of color who did not have the right to vote - not because they weren't eligible, but because no one had asked them. When we asked people to register to vote, they stepped up: The organization submitted more than 200,000 voter registrations in two years. Our ability to participate in government, to elect our leaders and to improve our lives, is contingent upon our ability to access the ballot. We know in our heart of hearts that voting is a sacred right - the fount from which all other rights flow. Now, it's time to live that truth through our actions. Stories from places like Randolph County should not discourage us, but instead motivate us to fight harder, march further, and shout louder: **No vote will go uncounted, no matter how hard they try.** To write the next chapter of our state's history, to build a brighter future for ourselves and our families, we have a lot of work to do. We have to reach out to those who have been disappointed again and again by promises made but never kept. We have to drive our friends and neighbors to the polls. We have to make sure every person we know can exercise their constitutional right to vote, and seek out those we don't know yet to prove that they matter to us, too. Because our campaign is a coalition that reaches across backgrounds, sharing our constant belief that all of our voices matter, and when we raise them in unison - we win. With your hands knocking, your feet marching, and your voices speaking out -from this moment to November and beyond -every Georgian we touch will understand the immense power of our vote. **[Now is the time to defend our values and protect the vulnerable, from Randolph County to Rabun County. Now is the time to stand in the gap, to lead the way, and to know that this democracy only works when we work for it. Chip in to join us.](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa9a3/34a064cc/d1dd8ab/70ef4dd/2783328637/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUwODE5MTgtZnVsbCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-august?refcode=IE081918-full")** Your right to vote is yours - no person or board of elections can take it away from you. It is a right worth fighting for, and we are going to give you a reason to use it in November. Together, we will speak out, we will stand up, and we will march all the way to the ballot box. Thank you for marching with me. \- Stacey Abrams

name: Jeremy Ring
subject: Let's invest in an innovation economy
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND APPROVED BY JEREMY RING, DEMOCRAT, FOR CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER
body:
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/o0NzSReFRS7jb3HbYze18XrxBPqZIjuh1KS3laPIxjU=/0FavqT5cN9dpmmnIMqovJ5f0ieA8Tx-Zg9B4DXJs-Lw=) Friends and Neighbors -- Throughout this campaign, I have talked a lot about building up an innovation centered economy here in Florida, one that creates more - and better - career opportunities in our great state. On the trail, I am often asked: _" How do we get there?" _ Well, during my ten years in the Florida Senate, I helped to lay the foundation for an innovation economy by creating and fostering initiatives such as the Florida Institute of Commercialization, which helps entrepreneurs with business fundamentals - i.e. writing business plans - and helps them to secure company funding to create the leading products and companies of tomorrow. And it worked. This big investment in innovation created many real, long-term high-paying jobs for Floridians. **The programs that I helped start during my time in the Senate have created an economic impact of over $1 BILLION for the state**. Now, it's time to take this investment in innovation to the next level. [Help Jeremy Get to the Cabinet. Chip in $20.18.](https://act.myngp.com/el/o0NzSReFRS7jb3HbYze18XrxBPqZIjuh1KS3laPIxjU=/jJ8ITIfpmwiCUBBXGfFJIQtNK43JA_iNIOMuewP-zsE=?am=20.18&recipientId=EsCT1N2SCLZJkIJRRTPlB2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&) As your next CFO, one of my top priorities would be using my experience in the tech sector and the State Senate to create, promote and implement forward thinking policies that would aim to both keep and create tech sector and other innovative centered jobs right here in the Sunshine State, fostering an innovation economy, ready made for the 21st century. **This investment in an innovation economy would help Florida set a national standard.** I'm ready to get right to work, to get the Cabinet working for you again, to invest _YOUR_ tax payer dollars in a way that will get real results, for all Floridians and for our wonderful state's future. But I have to get there first. **And I can 't do it without your help**. [**Can you contribute $20.18 or more today - and help me bring our innovative vision to the Cabinet?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/o0NzSReFRS7jb3HbYze18XrxBPqZIjuh1KS3laPIxjU=/jJ8ITIfpmwiCUBBXGfFJIQtNK43JA_iNIOMuewP-zsE=?am=20.18&recipientId=EsCT1N2SCLZJkIJRRTPlB2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&) Thank you for all of your support, **Jeremy Ring** | POLITICAL ADVERTISEMENT PAID FOR AND APPROVED BY JEREMY RING, DEMOCRAT, FOR CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER

Peter,

**One of the pleasures of being a candidate is getting to know your district.** I've seen the sunrise over farm pastures in Lee County, and the sunset over bluebonnet-covered hills in Brenham. I've met incredibly dedicated activists in every region of the the District, including places like La Grange and Bastrop and Sealy. And I've spent some time in Brookshire. **My first day of campaigning was in Brookshire** , on the Saturday after Thanksgiving. After spending the holiday with my Houston family, I set out with a petition and a list of registered voters and went door-to-door. My first signatures were from a teacher and her husband. I met a Republican businessman who applauded my old-fashioned approach but declined to sign. I got a couple of negative responses. And then **I met my first "undercover Democrat"** : a nice lady who said she didn't want to sign my petition because she might vote Republican. But she asked what I stood for, heard a few ideas and invited me inside for a Coke. And then, after a little more discussion, she said "I always vote Democrat" and signed my petition. [**We've come a long way, but we need your help to win this race. Please make a contribution today**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1950346/10201689/-2058851995?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YTc3NTkxYy04MmE0LWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DR__f5efFfZX-3GIJlmnhPru9GUhPX8MqeB9T4QqYD4=). **Saturday, I was back in Brookshire again, but this time not on my own**. Our regional organizer got us a room at the local library. We were joined by the county party chair, the Democratic nominee for county clerk, and a state house candidate. Volunteers came and received an overview, and some offered to organize their own volunteer events in coming weeks. We went out to knock on doors in a typical 100 degree summer afternoon. I got to visit a new neighborhood, and learn a little more about the people. **Brookshire is an important town for us**, in an important county, Waller County. It's about 30 minutes west of Houston, with over 5,000 people, and it is an amazingly diverse place, with near-equal populations of white, Hispanic, and African-American residents. Brookshire has historic neighborhoods and it has new neighbors -- including Amazon, which opened a warehouse there. **Brookshire is also in a county that has played a major role in local civil rights history.** Waller County is where the movement to seek justice for Sandra Bland began three years ago, when Sandra died in police custody following an improper traffic stop. Waller County is home to Prairie View A&M University, an historically black university, and the courageous work of Prairie View students who have had to repeatedly fight for and win the right to vote. Our campaign knows that it is the people who live in each community who know the most about what needs to be done. **By joining with the people of Brookshire and Waller County, and with the people of the other towns and regions of the Texas 10th, we are working towards a stronger community and a better Texas.** **And we'd love for you to join us.** Our organizer in Waller County, Jacob Aronowitz, can be reached at jacob@siegelfortexas.org. | **Partisan Attacks Weaken Public Policy & Undermine the Bipartisan Focus & Progress on the Opioid Epidemic** _In response to the Democratic Party Leaders’ statements that sadly turn our work on the opioid epidemic into a partisan issue, Campaign Manager Brittney Wilson released the following statement:_

**“Everyone who cares about the opioid epidemic has an obligation to point out that, until today, combating the epidemic had been a truly collaborative non-partisan issue. **

“In a deeply hypocritical statement, the Vermont Democratic Party failed to mention that pharmaceutical manufacturers have contributed thousands of dollars to the Vermont Democrats, and millions of dollars at the national level.

“The fact remains that pharmaceutical manufacturers make a wide range of life-saving medicine; have accepted the role that they and prescribers played in this issue; and are actively supporting efforts to reverse it. If the Vermont Democratic Party objects, they should call on 100% of their elected officials, candidates, committees and PACs to return all of the contributions they have received from pharmaceutical manufacturers in this, and all previous, cycles.

**“Putting campaign finances aside, the real issue is whether or not we’re going to let the Vermont Democratic Party pull the most challenging health crisis we face -- and the many years of non-partisan collaboration -- into the political gutter. **

“The Democratic Party’s statement should not be dismissed as just political theatre, election year politics, or DC-style politics -- or any other typical excuse. **This is very serious** and it should be called out for what it is: **Extremely corrosive.**

“It’s corrosive to the consensus required to do the difficult work of reversing the greatest public health epidemic of our time.

“It’s corrosive to the relationships necessary to solve other very complex challenges, like our eroding demographics, our national and global economic competitiveness and the economic security of every family in all 251 communities of our state.

“It’s corrosive to the integrity of the entire policymaking process itself.

**“Across multiple Administrations and Legislatures, Vermont’s non-partisan approach has delivered substantial progress.** As a result, Vermont’s response to the opioid epidemic is regarded as the most effective in the country. Governor Scott and the Secretary of Human Services were invited to testify earlier this year in front of a bipartisan congressional committee. And officials from other states have visited Vermont to learn how we’re making so much more progress than other states.

“Yes, there’s A LOT more to do to reverse the impacts of this crisis -- and the ability of future Administrations and Legislatures to build on Vermont’s success is now being undermined by a party that hopes to score a few quick, and dirty, political points. **We must not shrug this off as “routine party politics” because today, the severity of our largest public health crisis was used in a hypocritical attack in an attempt to bring people down. **

“Vermont has a history of being the moral compass of our country. It’s time to lead the nation, again. We all know that this is a time in our Nation’s history where doing what’s right -- putting principle ahead of politics -- is increasingly important. ** This is not a partisan issue -- it’s a human issue. It’s about families and communities. And we must do better.” ** ###

**IMPORTANT BACKGROUND: 19 MONTHS OF RESULTS & PROGRESS**
The fact is the unified commitment and non-partisan approach to this challenge has generated real, measurable results. **Real leaders know that protecting this non-partisan, unified, and effective, collaboration should always be a higher priority than election year gimmicks. **

Vermont is a leader in addressing this crisis. For 15 years collaboration and commitment have been the cornerstones of a unified effort that is, in fact, turning the tide. And Governor Scott, and the Legislature, have worked hard to build on this history, and significantly accelerate the results. For example, in just 19 months the Scott Administration, working with the Legislature, has achieved the following:

**Expanding Access to Treatment**
* Worked with partners and providers to expedite opening of the St Albans Hub (including opening of a temporary hub at Northwest Medical Center). These efforts supported the hard work of providers and treatment professionals across the state, helping bring the [Chittenden County treatment wait list to zero](https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=98f93e75d9&e=19cebfe173), which created the capacity statewide to get people into treatment quickly.
* [Secured an amendment](https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=0cf92e7997&e=19cebfe173) to Vermont’s Global Commitment to Health 1115 Demonstration waiver, allowing Medicaid to pay for inpatient residential treatment for addiction.
* Expanded Medication Assisted Treatment to a 120-day continuation at all Department of Corrections sites.
* After Maple Leaf Treatment Center in Underhill had to close its doors in February 2018, the Agency of Human Services partnered with Valley Vista and the Recovery House Network in Rutland to ensure there would not be a gap in inpatient treatment capacity.

**Improving Statewide Coordination**
* Created, [by Executive Order](https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=00b0355964&e=19cebfe173), the Opioid Coordination Council (OCC), with a mission to strengthen and expand Vermont’s response to the opioid crisis [through prevention, treatment, recovery and enforcement](https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=2470ca8b33&e=19cebfe173), while improving coordination across all state and local entities with a role in addressing the crisis.
* Created the new position of Director of Drug Prevention Policy to align the delivery of services within state government, oversee the OCC and work with communities across Vermont to implement strategies.
* Repurposed the Vermont Agency of Education’s Tobacco Use Prevention Coordinator position into a Substance Use Prevention Coordinator, allowing the State to address a wider range of prevention efforts in our schools.

**Expanding Substance Use Disorder Treatment Workforce**
* [Coordinated a statewide substance use disorder workforce summit in April 2017](https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=bb62007040&e=19cebfe173), hosting more than 150 health, education and policy stakeholders to collaborate on solutions to substance abuse disorders, with a focus on increasing professionals in the treatment and recovery fields.
* Working with the Secretary of State's Office of Professional Regulation (OPR), providers and industry stakeholders, [reformed administrative rules governing licensed treatment professionals](https://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=bb0ce1896e&e=19cebfe173). These reforms increased the efficiency of the licensing process, allowing for expansion of this sector of the workforce.

**Employment & Recovery**
* Launched a new initiative to have Vermont Department of Labor employment counselors make regular visits to all of Vermont’s recovery centers, providing services and guidance to help those in recovery find and keep jobs.

**Protecting Children Impacted by the Crisis**
* Proposed and passed investment of $500,000 for a child protection initiative within DCF, which supports parent-child contact and will help reduce the backlog of court cases involving children affected by the opioid epidemic.
* Proposed and passed increased investment in the guardian ad litem program, which supports children in the court system.

**Working to Reduce Supply**
* The Vermont Drug Task Force, in partnership with federal and local law enforcement, has concluded multiple significant drug trafficking investigations involving illegal opioids and other narcotics, resulting in the arrest of over 200 individuals for narcotics violations. These investigations targeted Vermonters responsible for distributing narcotics, including heroin, and facilitating the distribution of illegal drugs by out-of-state suppliers.
* The Department of Public Safety has secured a $1.3 million Department of Justice Grant to combat heroin trafficking in Vermont.
* In partnership with the Drug Enforcement Administration and local law enforcement, promote and participate in “Drug Take Back” days, which aim to provide a safe, convenient and responsible means of disposing of prescription drugs, including unused opioids. In the last 18 months, nearly nine tons of unused prescriptions, including opioids, have been safely disposed of by Vermonters.
* Establishing prescription drug disposal sites at all ten Vermont State Police Barracks.

**This positive progress -- and the non-partisan approach driving it forward -- is the real story here.** Governor Scott remains committed to reaching across the aisle and breaking down barriers to make progress. Everyone running for office should share this commitment.
**We will only eradicate this problem by putting petty politics aside and working together. **
###

The White House • August 20, 2018
## **The Day Ahead**
President Donald J. Trump hosts a Salute to the Heroes of ICE and CBP, recognizing the dangerous, important work of America’s border officers. **[Watch live at 3 p.m. ET](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwODIwLjkzODUxNjkxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDgyMC45Mzg1MTY5MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzczMzM2JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&100&&&https://www.whitehouse.gov/live/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d).**
## The life-saving missions of ICE
##
**From saving victims** from human smugglers, drug cartels, and criminal gangs to protecting American citizens from crime spilling across our border, President Trump understands the dangerous conditions our border officers face each day. To show his appreciation for their service, the President will highlight the life-saving missions of these heroes today in an event at the White House. **Even with widespread public support for ICE** (U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement), many Democrats have embraced the previously fringe position that the agency should be abolished. A recent POLITICO/Morning Consult poll revealed that even with the left’s recent push, only a quarter of Americans want ICE eliminated. **The heroes who protect our borders** are a vital piece of our national security. Getting rid of ICE would be devastating to the safety of American families, particularly those living in communities across the Southwestern United States. Our border officers are the first line of defense for these vulnerable Americans: During the 2017 fiscal year, ICE arrested more than 127,000 aliens with criminal convictions or charges. **President Trump promised** that he would secure our borders and keep our communities safe. Today, he is honoring the public servants who risk it all to help achieve that. **As Judge Brett Kavanaugh** continues to [**draw rave reviews**](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwODIwLjkzODUxNjkxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDgyMC45Mzg1MTY5MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4MzczMzM2JmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&103&&&https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/statements-support-judge-brett-kavanaugh-pour/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d) from the legal community and those who know him best, a few liberal Democrats are grasping at straws in their attempt to discredit his nomination. **In _National Review_** , David French highlights one particularly desperate—and shoddy—attempt, assisted by _The New York Times_. “There is no indication at all that the Times reached out to one of the key players in this whole drama,” French explains. “The Times got comment from [Democratic Senators] Chuck Schumer and Dianne Feinstein, but not from a key witness.” **“So I picked up the phone** and called him,” French writes. “Once again, the Left has taken a swing at one of the most qualified SCOTUS nominees in history. name: Amy Klobuchar
subject: Social Security's 83rd Anniversary
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Klobuchar for Minnesota
body:
Hi,
This month, we have something really special to celebrate: the 83rd anniversary of the signing of the Social Security Act.
Since 1935, Social Security has touched a countless number of lives. Almost **two-thirds** of American seniors depend on Social Security, and it has helped lift **more than 40 percent** of our seniors out of poverty.
The support Social Security provides is vital for **retirees, people with disabilities, and the surviving spouses and children of deceased workers** all over Minnesota and the country.
That's why I have consistently fought against risky schemes to privatize Social Security, and I will continue to do so. I'll also keep fighting to extend the lifespan of Social Security with legislation like the _Keeping our Social Security Promises Act,_ which I have co-sponsored.
**But I can 't do any of that without your help. [So to mark the 83rd anniversary of the enactment of the Social Security Act, will you add your name to tell Congress to protect Social Security and ensure it continues to guarantee a secure retirement for Americans well into the future?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=fFBHzVML9j3ArJGS2RX4FeWQjh2K6zLT)**
Thank you,
Amy

subject: We roll deep.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
Find out who is on Team Jealous.

| [](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37b18b/7ab0e2c4/7fd823a0/349b6f59/2542588136/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-by-the-people?refcode=OHQ08208A") Friend, Here at Team Jealous, we roll deep. How deep? Meet everyone who has joined us. _Vice President Joe Biden is on Team Jealous._ | | [](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37b18b/7ab0e2c4/7fd823a0/349b6f59/2542588136/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-by-the-people?refcode=OHQ08208A")
---

_"As a civil rights leader, community organizer, and businessman, Ben knows how to build an inclusive economy that works for everyone. Ben has the courage and heart to move Maryland forward."_
_Senator Bernie Sanders is on Team Jealous._
| [](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37b18b/7ab0e2c4/7fd823a0/349b6f59/2542588136/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-by-the-people?refcode=OHQ08208A")
---

_"With Ben as governor, we can make healthcare a right, not a privilege. We can create a minimum wage which is a living wage. We can stop the school-to-prison pipeline and end mass incarceration. We can make college tuition affordable, protect our environment and create good-paying jobs."_
_Senator Kamala Harris is on Team Jealous._
| [](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37b18b/7ab0e2c4/7fd823a0/349b6f59/2542588136/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-by-the-people?refcode=OHQ08208A")
---

_"Ben Jealous is a champion of working people. >From his days working as a community organizer and civil rights leader to his exceptional leadership of the NAACP, Ben has shown the courage to take on the biggest challenges we face."_
_Senator Cory Booker is on Team Jealous._
| [](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37b18b/7ab0e2c4/7fd823a0/349b6f59/2542588136/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-by-the-people?refcode=OHQ08208A")
---

_In an interview with The Washington Post, Senator Booker called Jealous:
"one of the most compelling voices of the X Generation" and said he is "all in" to do whatever Jealous needs to help him win the nomination and unseat Governor Hogan. Jealous is "a proven leader who brings an authentic voice," continued Booker, who described himself as a "raving fan."_
_Senator Elizabeth Warren is on Team Jealous._
| [](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37b18b/7ab0e2c4/7fd823a0/349b6f59/2542588136/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-by-the-people?refcode=OHQ08208A")
---

_"Ben Jealous is the kind of leader working families need to ensure we build an inclusive economy that works for the people, not special interests. As a civil rights leader and businessman, Ben has spent his life winning big battles on behalf of the working poor and middle-class. He will be a governor with the courage to fight back against Donald Trump's extreme agenda."_
_Maryland State Education Association is on Team Jealous._
| [](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37b18b/7ab0e2c4/7fd823a0/349b6f59/2542588136/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-by-the-people?refcode=OHQ08208A")
---

_"Our children need a new Maryland Promise that no matter their neighborhood, the state will make sure there's a strong public school in their community. Ben Jealous is the candidate with the values, the vision, and the coalition-building skills to make that promise a reality."_
_National Nurses United is on Team Jealous._
| [](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37b18b/7ab0e2c4/7fd823a0/349b6f59/2542588136/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-by-the-people?refcode=OHQ08208A")
---

_"Larry Hogan has repeatedly turned his back on the people of Maryland who desperately need new leadership in Annapolis. Ben Jealous is the bold, progressive, visionary leader Maryland needs to move forward in the 21st century."_
**And so are countless other progressive organizations, unions, and environmental groups, including:**
* Los Angeles Mayor Eric Garcetti
* New York Mayor Bill de Blasio
* Friends of the Earth
* NARAL Pro-Choice Maryland
* Democracy for America
* CASA in Action
* The Black Economic Alliance
* Congressman Elijah Cummings (and countless other Maryland elected officials)
* SEIU
* Maryland Working Families
* UNITE HERE! Local 7
* Dave Chappelle
* Ben & Jerry's co-founders Ben Cohen and Jerry Greenfield
* ATU International
* Communications Workers of America
* UFCW 27, 400, and 1994

...and the list goes on.
This is a campaign built by the people, for the people. We're not funded by shadowy corporate donors -- we're funded by you.
[**Donate $5 or more now if you're ready to fund the fight for a better, cleaner, more progressive Maryland.**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37b18b/7ab0e2c4/7fd823a0/349b6f59/2542588136/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-by-the-people?refcode=OHQ08208A")

Team Jealous

subject: You, Lorraine, Carol, Mary, Emily, Cathy, and Wendy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
You can read these 6 stories in 60 seconds.

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4a1e6/c1f7015/6a773f63/1141a4bb/3858644137/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-august?refcode=rtw_20180820_em-full&recurring=auto") Friend, we noticed you have not made a contribution to support Tina's campaign. We're counting on grassroots supporters like you to stop Trump from turning this seat red -- and I hope that after you read why these supporters stepped up below you'll be inspired to donate just like they were. _" I still cherish memories of growing up in Hibbing. Minnesota has always been a leader in education and thoughtful politics. It nurtured some great statesmen. Now we have a chance to recreate the future of the state with some wonderful women in the lead!"_
**-Lorraine D.** _" I've had the privilege of knowing and working with Tina, and hold her in the highest regard. It is essential that she continue to serve Minnesota in the U.S. Senate, so I'm digging deep to support her campaign!"_
**-Carol B.** _" I have full confidence in Tina's courage, ethics, and vision. She will fight for all of us."_
**-Mary H.** _" It is so important to protect immigrants, women's reproductive rights, fund public education, and work towards equitable health care for all."_
**-Emily D.** _" I think you are doing a good job for Minnesota in the Senate. I want Democrats to gain control of the House and Senate. We need to stand up to Trump and protect our democracy."_
**-Cathy F.** _" Tina is a progressive candidate who is not beholden to big money. She will do what is right for American citizens, not for the 1%."_
**-Wendy R.** **We 're facing right-wing billionaires, super PACs, and dark money organizations who will do everything they can to defeat us. And we're relying on grassroots supporters like you, Lorraine, Carol, Mary, Emily, Cathy, and Wendy to take them on.** That means the power to win, or lose, is in your hands. **[With the polls showing our lead at just four points, your contribution is the only thing that stands between Trump succeeding in turning this seat red or a big Democratic victory in November. Will you step up and make a contribution now?](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4a1e6/c1f7015/6a773f63/1141a4bb/3858644137/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-august?refcode=rtw_20180820_em-full&recurring=auto")** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [$10 »](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4a1e6/c1f7015/6a773f63/1141a4bc/3858644137/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-august?refcode=rtw_20180820_em-full-exp&express_lane=true&amount=10")
---

name: Zephyr Teachout
subject: The New York Times endorsement nailed the stakes of the office and this race.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Zephyr for NY
body:
I'm so honored to have this support.
#

Peter -
When I heard that the New York Times had endorsed our campaign last night, I was so overwhelmed that I literally dropped my phone.
Our team was in the office working together when the news broke, and we were shocked. We did not expect that they would be making such an unequivocal endorsement in this race right now.
After we took a moment to celebrate, we got right back to work because we know that this is crunch time. The primary is just three short weeks away, and we have a lot of work to do in that time to share our vision with voters. Can we count on you to join us to get across the finish line?
**[Make a $3 donation today to help keep our grassroots campaign going. With this endorsement, the political and financial establishment are likely to spend even more money against us now, so we need your help to fight back.](https://go.zephyrforny.com/page/m/4d780eca/6e822e2f/1ae5c415/41725cf4/4246934869/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA4MjAtMS1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-august?refcode=em180820-1-full")**
The New York Times endorsement nailed the stakes of the office and this race. Here's how they put it:
**" The most important choice facing New York voters this fall is whom they will pick as their next state attorney general. The office could be the last line of defense against an antidemocratic president, a federal government indifferent to environmental and consumer protection and a state government in which ethics can seem a mere inconvenience."**
That's exactly right. This is a crisis moment for our democracy, and we're facing threats on all fronts. It's why I've said all along that our next Attorney General must be completely independent from the political establishment and corporations she must be a check on.
Peter, I'm so honored to have this support. We've known all along that we need a constitutional law and anti-corruption expert as our next Attorney General. It's the reason I got in this race -- I knew that I could not sit on the sidelines while our democracy was under attack.
People said it was a longshot when we started, but now we have a real shot at winning this thing. We're where we are now thanks to so many supporters like you who powered our campaign from the start. You helped us grow this movement, and we cannot stop now.
So if you can, help us capitalize on this momentum by chipping in today:
**[https://go.zephyrforny.com/three-weeks](https://go.zephyrforny.com/page/m/4d780eca/6e822e2f/1ae5c415/41725cf4/4246934869/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA4MjAtMS1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-august?refcode=em180820-1-full")**
Together, we can win this, and when we do, we will make sure that the people get justice.
All my best,
Zephyr
**[DONATE](https://go.zephyrforny.com/page/m/4d780eca/6e822e2f/1ae5c415/41725cf4/4246934869/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA4MjAtMS1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-august?refcode=em180820-1-full")**

name: Todd Prieb
subject: Missing: Soapbox Speaker
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee
body:
Peter,
On Saturday, J.D. took his turn at the _Iowa State Fair Political Soapbox_ , a longstanding tradition where national presidential hopefuls and politicians from across the state address the State Fair crowd and answer questions from the audience.
J.D. talked about his experience growing up in the 4th District. He talked about his desire to help create a health care system, economy, and government that works for all Americans and not just those at the top. J.D. also addressed the growing concerns of farmers who have already seen their profits go down over the last few years and are now dealing with the backlash of punitive tariffs and the consolidation of big ag companies.
Steve King didn’t address any of these things at the Soapbox. Because, **as usual, King didn’t show up**. He didn’t show up for the Soapbox. He can’t be found at any of his offices when constituents come to address concerns. He doesn’t hold Town Halls to hear how voters feel on the issues. And he doesn’t show up for his committee meetings in the House. Maybe that’s part of the reason he’s so out of touch with the district—**he simply doesn’t show up anymore!**
**If Steve King doesn’t want to put in the time and effort to represent the people of Iowa’s 4th CD, let’s take that burden off his hands by retiring him from Congress.** [**_You can show up for the movement to dethrone Steve King by clicking here to chip in whatever you can comfortably afford today!_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1956284/10229243/1304303353?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZjhiMjVhYy1jMGE0LWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pKMg6vNfOakVlThsUA-KQbpHCHdfftnEdYvaPuowxFI=)
J.D. visited all 39 counties in this district in his car, and has duplicated that effort by visiting all 39 counties again in the campaign RV, “Sioux City Sue.”
So, what’s next for J.D. and Team Scholten?
We’re doing what Steve King won’t do. You’ll see us in all 39 counties _again_ for Town Hall style events before the election on Nov. 6th because, as J.D. likes to say, “ _You can’t fake showing up!_ ” **The people of this district, and this country, deserve to have representatives who work as hard as they do. **
We’ll be announcing the schedule for the latest tour soon. [**_In the meantime, we need you to show up for us by kicking in what you can to help keep our RV and this campaign rolling!_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1956285/10229244/1304303353?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZjhiMjVhYy1jMGE0LWU4MTEtYmNlNy0wMDBkM2ExMmI4MDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pKMg6vNfOakVlThsUA-KQbpHCHdfftnEdYvaPuowxFI=)
Thank you for your continued support.
Todd Prieb
Deputy Campaign Manager
Team Scholten

body:
Derek, the contrast between Tim Kaine's campaign and Corey Stewart's has never been starker than it was this month. ---------------------------------------------------------------------
| | \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Derek, the contrast between Tim Kaine's campaign and Corey Stewart's has never been starker than it was this month.

**When we were approaching the one-year anniversary of the horrible white supremacist rally in Charlottesville:**
* \-- Tim asked his supporters to take a pledge to stand up against hate, bringing thousands of people from all over the country together to send a message: _Hate is not a Virginian value._

* \-- And Corey Stewart doubled down on his comments from last year agreeing with President Trump that there were good people on both sides in Charlottesville and saying it's time for us to blithely "move on" from that hate-filled rally which cost three Virginians their lives.

body:

---
| | This is news you don't want to see so close to a big election: _"The numbers show that, as a share of the electorate [in Florida],**Democratic Party registrations have fallen about 2 percentage points** since the 2016 election (to roughly 37 percent). Registered Republicans have held steady at 35 percent."_ We're eight days out from the Florida Primary. We're just a little over 80 days out from the General Election. **If we want to win up and down the ticket, we have to reach every Democratic voter we possibly can.** [**We need to raise $10,000 before Friday for our Get Out the Vote Fund. Can you chip in $5 or more?**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f43/622b7b5b/1c6e9862/307b929b/1287044430/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse180820?refcode=dwse180820_m&recurring=true&amount=5") Florida elections were always going to be close this year. These numbers seem to bear that out. We cannot take a single vote for granted. If we lose ground in November, Republicans will push ahead with their budget which cuts Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security. We will almost certainly lose the Affordable Care Act. Funding for Planned Parenthood and clinics that provide reproductive care could disappear. We need to do everything we can to Get Out the Vote. The Koch Brothers have millions in cash on hand, and they'll use it to boost Republican turnout. WE NEED TO PUSH BACK AND I CAN'T DO THAT WITHOUT YOU! [**Give today.**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f43/622b7b5b/1c6e9862/307b929b/1287044430/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse180820?refcode=dwse180820_m&recurring=true&amount=5") Thank you. Debbie

name: Tim Ryan
subject: Vote YES for TJX!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for Congress
body:
It's not yet November, but tomorrow there's an incredibly important election here in Lordstown. On the ballot: A very consequential measure to move Northeast Ohio's economy forward. | |
---
Folks, It's not yet November, but tomorrow there's an incredibly important election here in Lordstown. On the ballot: A very consequential measure to move Northeast Ohio's economy forward. **In a few short hours, Lordstown residents will vote on a referendum that could allow TJX to build a $170 million HomeGoods regional distribution center in the village._It is critically important that this ballot measure passes. _** **A YES vote** on the referendum would keep zoning changes to the property in question in place and the project on track. **A majority NO vote** likely would kill the project as it would overturn the required zoning changes. Approving this would be a huge boom for the Valley -- creating and supporting _**thousands**_ of good paying jobs that will support local families and Lordstown Schools. Representing Ohio's 13th Congressional District, which includes the Village of Lordstown, has been the greatest honor of my life. I've always fought for and wanted what is best for my constituents. There's not a doubt in my mind that voting YES tomorrow is the right thing to do for our neighbors, our community, and our region. That is why -- from meetings at my office to pounding the pavements of Lordstown, to dozens of phone calls directly with the CEO of HomeGoods -- I've been so invested in this project over the course of these last months. This is the opportunity of a lifetime, we can't let it slip away. **I hope you'll please consider this and vote YES tomorrow,****Tuesday,****August 21st if you are able. **[Click here to find your polling place.](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a926f/5486deaa/291aacb0/dcf988c/2336865157/VEsH/ "https://trumbull.oh-vote.org/VoterView/PollingPlaceSearch.do") Thank you, \-- Tim

name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: Celebrating our Second Birthday!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution
body:
Friends,

This week marks two revolutionary years here at Our Revolution. Now 600 Our Revolution local groups are building independent political power across the United States and around the world. Click here to see just how much our movement has grown since we started two years ago.

https://ourrevolution.com/year-two/?utm_source=ge180820

"Getting involved with Our Revolution means joining hands, it means connecting with like-minded individuals. Local groups plan events, mobilize others, and take initiative--organizing locally, we build each group for the long term. It does not mean simply showing up to protests. It means creating power by running together in the marathon that is our existence." - Our Revolution Minnesota

For two years, we've empowered working people to organize locally and elect progressive champions who will stand up for all of us. Over 100 Our Revolution endorsed candidates have won races from school board to city council, county commission to Congress, many of whom had never run for office before. These campaigns are inspiring people who had given up on politics by proving that the grassroots can dismantle the power of any establishment politician, no matter how fancy their title or how big their bank account.

"Our great accomplishment happened recently in 2018 when we joined with Our Revolution Contra Costa, Richmond Progressive Alliance, Democratic Socialists of America, unions, and other grassroots groups to lead a people-powered, corporate-free campaign to support Jovanka Beckles for the California Assembly. We learned that every vote counts and helped Jovanka move from 6th place to winning the primary election by only a few hundred votes." - Our Revolution East Bay

The political revolution we are building is not confined to "blue" states or "red" states, rural areas or cities. This movement is about working families and everyday people around the world who are demanding a fair shot at a better life. We are fighting for something bigger than the next election -- we are fighting for a social democracy that lifts up each and every one of us, Black, brown, or white.

Click here to join us in celebrating Our Revolution's second birthday.

https://ourrevolution.com/year-two/?utm_source=ge180820

In solidarity,

Nina Turner
President

name: Kathy Manning
subject: Update from the trail
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kathy Manning for Congress
body:
|
---

| | Another packed month has flown by! As summer winds down and the Fall election season is starting to heat up, we're gearing up for the final leg of this race! I wanted to make sure you were up to date on our campaign over this past month. But first, I really cannot thank you enough for all the hard work you've done so far. This journey has been incredible. With every person I meet, with every story I hear, I become more convinced than ever that families in this district are ready for a change -- and they're willing to fight for it, too. Talking, listening, and discussing problems is how we move forward together. We've continued to travel across the district holding meet and greets, "Coffee with Kathy" events, and meeting voters where they are -- their homes, businesses, diners, coffee shops, place of worship, and more. | [](https://manning.bsd.net/page/m/654279cc/e3b7d3/5dc2fff8/2555236b/206330624/VEsH/ "https://manning.bsd.net/e180819a2")
---
I'm always honored that people are willing to share their personal stories with me and I love discussing how we can solve the problems that touch their lives. These coffees, meet and greets, and conversations mean so much to me that we're now bringing the concept to our online team, too.
| [**Let me know what issues you talk about with your family over coffee.

Take the survey >>**](https://manning.bsd.net/page/m/654279cc/e3b7d3/5dc2fff8/2555236b/206330624/VEsE/ "https://manning.bsd.net/e180819a2")
---
We've put a lot more miles on my car this month! From Pig Pickin's and visiting with small businesses in Jamestown to the Woodleaf Tomato Festival in Rowan County, I've had a great time visiting with voters over good food, good music, and good times.
[](https://manning.bsd.net/page/m/654279cc/e3b7d3/5dc2fff8/2555236b/206330624/VEsF/ "https://manning.bsd.net/e180819a2")
---
Our ground game grew even bigger this past month! We are proud to have built the largest grassroots campaign this district has ever seen -- and we're not letting our foot off the gas!
We held another incredibly successful Organizing Day of Action to mark 100 days until the General Election. Our team set a goal of contacting 2,000 voters in just a single day. But we didn't hit that goal -- we CRUSHED it! In the end, we recruited **100 volunteers** to contact **more than 3,000 voters** and **raised $3,200 online in just a day!**
[](https://manning.bsd.net/page/m/654279cc/e3b7d3/5dc2fff8/2555236b/206330624/VEsC/ "https://manning.bsd.net/e180819a2")
---
Without your support, none of this would be possible. Every day, I am blown away by some new goal this team has hit, or some new accomplishment we've achieved. This fall is going to be tough, but I can't wait to see what we achieve. And together, I know we have the power to come out ahead in November.
Fired up after reading all these exciting campaign updates? Me too! [**Sign up to join our grassroots volunteer team and help us keep the momentum going.**](https://manning.bsd.net/page/m/654279cc/e3b7d3/5dc2fff8/2555236a/206330624/VEsD/ "https://manning.bsd.net/e180819ld")
Thank you for everything you've done. Until next time.
---

---

name: Ralph
subject: want to hear from you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Way Ahead The Way Ahead
body:
| | |
---
Let me be clear, friends. Untraceable, 3D-printed guns pose a dangerous threat to our community.
The Trump administration gave the green light to the public release of 3D printer blueprints for guns. The move would have allowed basically anyone to make untraceable guns--including AR-15s--from the anonymity of their own home.
Make no mistake: Anyone unable to pass a simple gun background check should not be able to bypass gun safety laws. We have checks and accountability so that no one can walk into a store and then commit violent acts in our community, let alone possess an untraceable weapon.
That's why I'm proud that Virginia joined a lawsuit that has temporarily blocked the release of these blueprints. But without permanent action, these dangerous blueprints could still become widely available. **That's why I need to hear from 5,000 of you by midnight on this urgent question: **
| | **Should we ban 3D-printed guns?**

subject: Real stories from the ground
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:

| | Hi Peter. I'm Serena. I'm the grassroots fundraising director on Team Sherrod. **And these are two stories from two of our event hosts on the ground.** Ruth lives in Yellow Springs, Ohio. Her granddaughter was born with cerebral palsy. Because she was born overseas to a military family, the standards of testing and treatment were different, meaning that Ruth's granddaughter had trouble getting the care she needed in the U.S. Ruth called everyone she could think of to get help navigating the complex healthcare systems. **The first office to help her was Sherrod's.** Richard lives in Upper Arlington, Ohio. He's 100 years old, a veteran, and when he votes, he generally votes Republican. But not this year -- this year, **he's voting for Sherrod.** I've heard hundreds of these stories around the state. Stories of how Sherrod's helped people find health insurance, helped veterans access benefits, helped businesses get up and running... the list goes on and on. It's one thing to see Sherrod giving a speech or an interview and think "he's got some good ideas." But when you're on the ground, hearing how Sherrod's work actually touches people's lives, it's incredibly powerful. It's why I love my job. Sherrod doesn't just talk about problems -- he solves them. And with all the absolute dysfunction going on in Washington D.C., Sherrod's is a voice we can't afford to lose. [**Kimberly told me that the August goal for the online team is $470,000 -- and with the month creeping toward an end, we're about $110,000 short. \-------FORWARDED MESSAGE-------

From: Mark Warner
Subject: Corey Stewart

Derek, the contrast between Tim Kaine's campaign and Corey Stewart's has never been starker than it was this month.

**When we were approaching the one-year anniversary of the horrible white supremacist rally in Charlottesville:**
* \-- Tim asked his supporters to take a pledge to stand up against hate, bringing thousands of people from all over the country together to send a message: _Hate is not a Virginian value._

* \-- And Corey Stewart doubled down on his comments from last year agreeing with President Trump that there were good people on both sides in Charlottesville and saying it's time for us to blithely "move on" from that hate-filled rally which cost three Virginians their lives.

[Will you show Corey Stewart that his attempts to sow hate and division won't win in Virginia by chipping in $5 to Tim's campaign today?](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f12a4/3e0906/6c8c00e4/3e36a9a3/384666921/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-2018-general-election-fund?refcode=20180820_2_FR_TEAM_Warner-FWD-LDND&amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=5+-+Will+you+show+Corey+Stewart+that+his+att&utm_campaign=20180820_2_FR_TEAM_Warner-FWD-LDND&source=20180820_2_FR_TEAM_Warner-FWD-LDND")
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

To put it simply, Derek: **Tim 's main priority is to unite folks, and Corey's is to divide us.**

This year's election is bigger than Virginia - it's about proving that hatred and division aren't winning campaign strategies. And we can't prove that without your help.

[So if you're with us, donate whatever you can afford to Tim's campaign today >>](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f12a4/3e0906/6c8c00e4/3e36a9a6/384666921/VEsHBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-2018-general-election-fund?refcode=20180820_2_FR_TEAM_Warner-FWD-LDND&amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=10+-+So+if+yoursquore+with+us+donate++if+cont&utm_campaign=20180820_2_FR_TEAM_Warner-FWD-LDND&source=20180820_2_FR_TEAM_Warner-FWD-LDND")

Thanks for being on Team Kaine,

_Mark Warner_

P.S. You and I both know things are only going to get uglier from here, so help make sure Tim can run a positive campaign from now through November by [making a recurring donation through Election Day >>](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f12a4/3e0906/6c8c00e4/3e36a9a5/384666921/VEsHBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-2018-recurring-fund?refcode=20180820_2_FR_TEAM_Warner-FWD-LDND&amount=25&recurring=4&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=11+-+making+a+recurring+donation+through+Elec&utm_campaign=20180820_2_FR_TEAM_Warner-FWD-LDND&source=20180820_2_FR_TEAM_Warner-FWD-LDND")

body:
We wanted to make sure you saw this:

Peter -
We wanted to make sure you saw our big news: **The New York Times has endorsed Zephyr for Attorney General.**
They know that Zephyr's expertise in constitutional law and anti-corruption are what this moment demands, and by rejecting corporate money, she will be truly independent. But now the corporations who know that Attorney General Teachout would be their worst nightmare are likely to spend even more trying to stop us.
**[Can you make a $3 donation right now? This is important. We cannot do this alone.](https://go.zephyrforny.com/page/m/4d780ec9/6e822e2f/1aefafd3/41725390/2698920361/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA4MjAtMi1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-august?refcode=em180820-2-full")**
-Team Zephyr

* * *
#
Peter -
When I heard that the New York Times had endorsed our campaign last night, I was so overwhelmed that I literally dropped my phone.
Our team was in the office working together when the news broke, and we were shocked. We did not expect that they would be making such an unequivocal endorsement in this race right now.
After we took a moment to celebrate, we got right back to work because we know that this is crunch time. The primary is just three short weeks away, and we have a lot of work to do in that time to share our vision with voters. Can we count on you to join us to get across the finish line?
**[Make a $3 donation today to help keep our grassroots campaign going. With this endorsement, the political and financial establishment are likely to spend even more money against us now, so we need your help to fight back.](https://go.zephyrforny.com/page/m/4d780ec9/6e822e2f/1aefafd3/41725390/2698920361/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA4MjAtMi1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-august?refcode=em180820-2-full")**
The New York Times endorsement nailed the stakes of the office and this race. Here's how they put it:
**" The most important choice facing New York voters this fall is whom they will pick as their next state attorney general. The office could be the last line of defense against an antidemocratic president, a federal government indifferent to environmental and consumer protection and a state government in which ethics can seem a mere inconvenience."**
That's exactly right. This is a crisis moment for our democracy, and we're facing threats on all fronts. It's why I've said all along that our next Attorney General must be completely independent from the political establishment and corporations she must be a check on.
Peter, I'm so honored to have this support. We've known all along that we need a constitutional law and anti-corruption expert as our next Attorney General. It's the reason I got in this race -- I knew that I could not sit on the sidelines while our democracy was under attack.
People said it was a longshot when we started, but now we have a real shot at winning this thing. We're where we are now thanks to so many supporters like you who powered our campaign from the start. You helped us grow this movement, and we cannot stop now.
So if you can, help us capitalize on this momentum by chipping in today:
**[https://go.zephyrforny.com/three-weeks](https://go.zephyrforny.com/page/m/4d780ec9/6e822e2f/1aefafd3/41725390/2698920361/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA4MjAtMi1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-august?refcode=em180820-2-full")**
Together, we can win this, and when we do, we will make sure that the people get justice.
All my best,
Zephyr
**[DONATE](https://go.zephyrforny.com/page/m/4d780ec9/6e822e2f/1aefafd3/41725390/2698920361/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA4MjAtMi1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-august?refcode=em180820-2-full")**
